info.name: Blood Platform API
info.description:
Blood Bank Management Platform — import this file into Postman.

All responses follow the generic ApiResponse wrapper:
{
  "success": true|false,
  "message": "...",
  "data": { ... },      // omitted on error
  "errors": { ... },    // omitted on success
  "timestamp": "..."
}

--- HOW TO TEST ---
1. Run Login (Admin) first — token is auto-saved to {{token}}
2. Auth endpoints: use {{authUrl}} (port 8081)
3. Hospital endpoints: use {{hospitalUrl}} (port 8083)

Default seed admin: admin@blood.com / Admin1!

variable.description: JWT Bearer token — auto-set by any Login request.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
AuthController — /api/auth

Public endpoints: /register and /login
Protected endpoint: /me (requires Bearer token)

request.description:
Register a new DONOR. hospitalId is not required for DONOR or ADMIN roles.

Password rules: minimum 8 characters, at least one number.

All new users start with status PENDING_APPROVAL — they cannot login until an admin approves them.

Success (201):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Registration successful. Awaiting admin approval.",
  "data": {
    "userId": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "DONOR",
    "accountStatus": "PENDING_APPROVAL"
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (400 — weak password):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "errors": { "password": "Password must be at least 8 characters and contain at least one number" },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (409 — duplicate email):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Email already registered: john@blood.com",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Register a CLINICIAN or OFFICER. hospitalId is REQUIRED for these roles — the system validates that the hospital exists.

Change role to OFFICER for a blood bank officer.

Error (400 — missing hospitalId):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "errors": { "hospitalId": "hospitalId is required for role CLINICIAN" },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (400 — unknown hospital):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Hospital not found with id: 999",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Login as the default seed admin. Token is auto-saved to {{token}}.

Run this first before using any Admin — User Management endpoints.

Seed credentials:
  Email   : admin@blood.com
  Password: Admin1!

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful.",
  "data": {
    "token": "eyJ...",
    "type": "Bearer",
    "userId": 17,
    "name": "System Admin",
    "email": "admin@blood.com",
    "role": "ADMIN",
    "accountStatus": "ACTIVE"
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (401 — wrong credentials):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Invalid email or password",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (403 — account not active):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Account pending approval. Please wait for an administrator to approve your account.",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (403 — account locked after 5 failed attempts):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Account is temporarily locked. Try again later.",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Login as a non-admin user (DONOR, CLINICIAN, OFFICER). Token is auto-saved to {{token}}.

The account must be ACTIVE (approved by admin) before login works.
Replace the email/password with the credentials of the user you registered.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful.",
  "data": {
    "token": "eyJ...",
    "type": "Bearer",
    "userId": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "DONOR",
    "accountStatus": "ACTIVE"
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Returns the profile of whoever is currently logged in (based on {{token}}).

Use this to verify your token is working and to see your own user details.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User profile retrieved.",
  "data": {
    "id": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "DONOR",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "createdAt": "..."
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (401 — no token or expired):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Token expired",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

item.name: Admin — User Management
item.description:
AdminController — /api/admin/users

ALL endpoints in this folder require an ADMIN token.
Run 'Login (Admin)' first to set {{token}}.

Typical workflow:
1. Login (Admin)
2. Get Pending Users — note the user id
3. Approve User — paste the id into the URL
4. Optionally deactivate or change role

request.description:
List every user on the platform across all roles and statuses. ADMIN only.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "All users retrieved.",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 17,
      "name": "System Admin",
      "email": "admin@blood.com",
      "role": "ADMIN",
      "status": "ACTIVE",
      "createdAt": "..."
    },
    {
      "id": 25,
      "name": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@blood.com",
      "role": "DONOR",
      "status": "PENDING_APPROVAL",
      "createdAt": "..."
    }
  ],
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (403 — not an admin):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Access Denied",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

List only users with status PENDING_APPROVAL. ADMIN only.

Use this to find which users are waiting to be approved. Copy the id from the response and use it in 'Approve User'.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Pending users retrieved.",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 25,
      "name": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@blood.com",
      "role": "DONOR",
      "status": "PENDING_APPROVAL",
      "createdAt": "..."
    }
  ],
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Approve a PENDING_APPROVAL user — sets their status to ACTIVE so they can login.

Replace '25' in the URL with the actual user id from 'Get Pending Users'.

Publishes a Kafka audit event: user-approved.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User approved successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "DONOR",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "createdAt": "..."
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (404 — user not found):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "User not found",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Suspend a user — sets their status to SUSPENDED.

Replace '25' in the URL with the actual user id.

The user's existing session is invalidated immediately on their next request (no need to wait for token expiry).

Cannot suspend the last active ADMIN — returns 409.

Publishes a Kafka audit event: user-deactivated.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User deactivated successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "DONOR",
    "status": "SUSPENDED",
    "createdAt": "..."
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (409 — last active admin):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "At least one active administrator required",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

query.description: Valid values: DONOR | CLINICIAN | OFFICER | ADMIN

request.description:
Change a user's role. Replace '25' with the actual user id and set newRole to the desired role.

Valid roles: DONOR, CLINICIAN, OFFICER, ADMIN

Cannot demote the last active ADMIN — returns 409.

Publishes a Kafka audit event: role-changed.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User role updated successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 25,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@blood.com",
    "role": "OFFICER",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "createdAt": "..."
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (409 — last active admin):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "At least one active administrator required",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

item.name: Hospitals
item.description:
HospitalController — /api/hospitals (port 8083)

GET endpoints: any authenticated user
POST / PUT / DELETE: ADMIN role required

Run Login (Admin) first to set {{token}}.

Workflow:
1. Login (Admin)
2. Register Hospital
3. Get Hospitals to see the list
4. Deactivate Hospital when needed

request.description:
Register a new hospital. ADMIN only.

All five fields are required. Duplicate name+city combination is rejected with 409.

Success (201):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Hospital registered successfully",
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Lagos General Hospital",
    "address": "1 Marina Road",
    "state": "Lagos",
    "city": "Lagos",
    "contact": "+2341234567",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "createdAt": "..."
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (400 — missing fields):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "errors": {
    "name": "Hospital name is required",
    "city": "City is required"
  },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (409 — duplicate name+city):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "A hospital named 'Lagos General Hospital' already exists in Lagos",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Get a single hospital by id. Any authenticated user. Replace 1 with the actual hospital id.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Hospital retrieved",
  "data": { "id": 1, "name": "Lagos General Hospital", ... },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (404):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Hospital not found with id: 1",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Deactivate a hospital (soft delete) — sets status to INACTIVE. ADMIN only.

Replace 1 with the actual hospital id.

Deactivated hospitals no longer appear in the active list or transfer dropdowns. Historical records are preserved.

Publishes a Kafka event: hospital-deactivated.

Success (200):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Hospital deactivated successfully",
  "data": { "id": 1, "status": "INACTIVE", ... },
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Error (404):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Hospital not found with id: 1",
  "timestamp": "..."
}

Hard DELETE is always blocked — returns 409. Use Deactivate Hospital instead.

This protects referential integrity with linked users, inventory, and transfer records.

Response (409):
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Deactivate the hospital instead of deleting it. Hard deletion is blocked to preserve referential integrity with linked users and inventory.",
  "timestamp": "..."
}
